conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
Sorry to hear that. Can you tell me a bit about what's been going on in your life recently? What's been taking up most of your time?
Honestly, work's been a grind lately. Been putting in 12-hour shifts and trying to keep up with paperwork.
I see. It sounds like you're feeling pretty burnt out. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine or habits since things have picked up at work?
Yeah, my sleep schedule's gone haywire. I'm getting maybe four hours of sleep a night and it's affecting everything.
That does sound challenging. Can you tell me more about what's been going through your mind lately? What are some of the thoughts or feelings that have been sticking with you?
Honestly, it feels like guilt's eating away at me. I'm constantly thinking about all these things I should be doing but aren't getting to.
It sounds like you're feeling a sense of responsibility weighing on your mind. Have you noticed any changes in your energy levels or motivation?
No, actually, the opposite. I've got way too much energy and it's making me restless. Can't seem to sit still for two seconds.
That can be overwhelming. What do you think is driving this restlessness? Is there anything specific that's getting you worked up?
Honestly, I just feel like I'm stuck in a rut. Don't know how to get out of it or what to prioritize anymore.
It sounds like you're feeling pretty stuck and unsure about where to go from here. That can be really frustrating. Can you tell me more about your relationships with family and friends lately?
Yeah, they're all trying to help but I just feel like I'm a burden right now. Don't want to dump my problems on them.
It sounds like you're feeling pretty isolated despite having people who care about you. Have you noticed any changes in your ability to concentrate or make decisions?
Yeah, it's like my brain's a jumble of thoughts and I can't focus on anything for more than two seconds.
That does sound challenging. What are some things that used to bring you joy or help you relax before all this started?
Music, actually. But even that feels like too much effort now.
It sounds like you're feeling pretty disconnected from activities that used to give you pleasure. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or eating habits lately?
Yeah, my stomach's been growling but I just don't have the energy to cook anything decent.
That can be really tough. What are some things you think you could do to start taking care of yourself better and getting back to the things that bring you joy?
Honestly, I just feel like I need a reset button or something. Don't know where to start.

It sounds like you're feeling pretty overwhelmed and uncertain about how to move forward. That's okay. We can work on breaking it down together. What do you think are some things that might be helpful for you right now?
I don't know, man. Just being able to get out of bed in the morning without feeling like I'm going to crawl back into bed would be a win.
That sounds like a great place to start. Let's work on prioritizing some small steps that can help you build momentum and feel more grounded. Does that sound okay to you?
Yeah, I guess so.
Okay, let's take it one step at a time. What do you think is the most important thing for us to focus on in this session today?
Just getting some perspective, I guess. Feeling like I'm stuck and don't know how to move forward.
I get that. Sometimes just having someone to talk through things with can be really helpful. Let's keep going then. What do you think is the most challenging part of your daily routine right now?
Definitely getting out of bed in the morning. The guilt and anxiety just hit me like a brick wall.
It sounds like that's a big hurdle to overcome. Have you noticed any patterns or triggers that might be contributing to this feeling?
Yeah, it seems like every day is different but somehow they all feel like a struggle.

Okay, so it sounds like there are some underlying issues at play here. Let's keep exploring what might be driving these feelings and see if we can identify any patterns or themes that might help us address them more effectively.
Yeah, okay, I guess that makes sense.
Great! So let's start by talking about your daily routine and seeing if there are any areas where you feel like things are getting in the way of you doing what you want to do.
Yeah, definitely. Like I said, sleep's been a real struggle lately.
Okay, so let's focus on that then. Can you tell me more about your sleep habits? How many hours are you sleeping each night and how are you feeling when you wake up in the morning?
Like I said, maybe four hours of sleep. And honestly, it feels like I'm just waking up to start stressing again.
Okay, so it sounds like you're not really getting a chance to recharge or feel rested before the day starts. Have you noticed any changes in your mood or emotional state since this started?
Yeah, I'd say I'm feeling pretty down most of the time now.
It sounds like you're struggling with some persistent feelings of sadness or hopelessness. Can you tell me more about that?
Yeah, just feels like nothing's going right and it's all my fault.
Okay, so it sounds like there might be some shame or self-blame mixed in with the feelings of sadness. Let's work on reframing those thoughts and finding ways to practice self-compassion.
Yeah, that would help I think.
Great! So let's start by acknowledging that everyone has struggles and it's okay not to be okay. Can you tell me more about what's been going through your mind lately?
Honestly, just feels like everything's falling apart and I'm the one who's always fixing things for others but can't fix anything myself.

Okay, so it sounds like there might be some feelings of powerlessness or inadequacy mixed in with the guilt. Let's work on building your sense of self-efficacy and finding ways to take small steps towards making positive changes.
Yeah, that would help a lot I think.
Great! So let's start by setting some small goals for yourself and working towards them. Can you tell me what some things are that you'd like to accomplish or achieve in the next few days?
Honestly, just getting out of bed without wanting to crawl back into bed would be a win.
Okay, so let's start with that then! What small steps can we take together to make that happen?
